steps.title: Two ways to set your language
steps.description: AhaSlides lets you set two different languages: the presenter interface language (what you see), and the audience display language (what participants see on their phones). Let's walk through both.

steps.title: Change the presenter interface language
steps.description: Click the globe icon in the top-right corner to change the language of the AhaSlides interface — menus, buttons, and labels — for you as the presenter.

action.hint: Click the globe icon to open the language menu

steps.title: Pick your interface language
steps.description: A dropdown menu lists all 27 supported interface languages. Select the one you want — the presenter app will switch instantly.

steps.title: Now set the audience language
steps.description: The audience display language is found inside any presentation's Settings. Click 'New presentation' to open the editor — then we'll show you where to find it.

steps.title: Start with a Poll slide
steps.description: Every presentation can have its own display language. Click 'Poll' to add a slide — then we'll head straight to the language settings.

action.hint: Click Poll to add a slide

steps.title: Slide added — heading to Settings
steps.description: The Poll slide is in your deck. Now let's open Settings to set the audience display language.

steps.title: Open the presentation Settings
steps.description: Click the Settings icon on the right-hand panel. This is where you control how the presentation behaves for your audience.

action.hint: Click Settings to open the settings panel

steps.title: Settings is loading
steps.description: The Settings panel is opening — you'll see options for your entire presentation.

steps.title: Go to Presentation language
steps.description: The Settings panel is open. Click 'Presentation language' in the left menu to access the audience language option.

action.hint: Click Presentation language in the menu

steps.title: Navigating to Presentation language
steps.description: Navigating to the Presentation language tab — here you'll find the dropdown for your audience's language.

steps.title: Choose the audience display language
steps.description: Use the dropdown to pick the language your audience will see on their devices — questions, answer options, and the join screen all update instantly. AhaSlides supports 32 display languages.

steps.title: Language saved — close Settings
steps.description: The language is saved automatically. Click the X to close Settings and head back to the editor.

action.hint: Click X to close the Settings modal

steps.title: Preview the audience experience
steps.description: Click Preview to see exactly what your audience will see when you go live — including the language you just set.

steps.title: This is your audience's view
steps.description: Your audience sees AhaSlides in the language you chose — questions, answer options, and the join screen all appear in that language on their phones.

steps.title: All set!
steps.description: That's it — your audience display language is saved automatically. Combine it with the presenter interface language to run fully bilingual sessions. Click Present when you're ready to go live.
